summary: wellnpm ships XMRig Monero cryptominer with hardcoded attacker wallet
details: The package's declared `main` is a 24MB ELF Android binary named `launch` which is XMRig (libuv, RandomX, hashvault references, /system/bin/linker64 dynamic loader). The shipped `config.json` is preconfigured to mine to `pool.hashvault.pro:80` under wallet address `4AUTfJGTvT8A98PSE99EunfrnUbkWe9vU3FN3qsABQLP5aPKQuvWS5sCaD9WaXFQUqNtDLb9dcxiBgJiWCfjvDXp5ptWDYG`. `npm start` runs the miner directly. The `deploy` script installs `libuv.so` into `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/lib`, chmod+x's helper shell scripts `xmr` and `edit`, and copies them into `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin` so the miner runs from the user's `$PATH` — persistence in the user's Termux shell environment. Package metadata is a thin cover story: name `wellnpm`, placeholder author `Your Name <wellenterprise81.gmail.com>`, generic description, keywords `job`/`worker` — none disclose cryptomining. Installing or running this package hijacks the host's CPU and electricity to mine Monero to an attacker-controlled wallet.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
